topic: Teichmüller family — German parish and biographical archive research
primary_source: Neue Deutsche Biographie (NDB), vol. 26 (2016), p. 6*–7*, Teichmüller, Gustav

status_change.finding: Hans–Gustav sibling relationship confirmed by NDB 2016 peer-reviewed genealogy section

hans_teichmueller.occupation: Jurist, Officer, Justice of the Peace / District Judge (22nd Judicial District)
hans_teichmueller.notes: Third son per TSHA. NDB confirms brothers: Gustav (1832) and Wilhelm (1834) were older. Emigrated to Texas 1856 after father's death.

august_wilhelm_teichmueller.death_cause: Unknown; death caused family financial hardship
august_wilhelm_teichmueller.occupation: Seconde-Lieutenant (Zweiter Leutnant), Schwarzen Corps des Majors Olfermann, Brunswick army
august_wilhelm_teichmueller.source: NDB 26 (2016) p. 6*, genealogy section of Gustav Teichmüller article
august_wilhelm_teichmueller.note_on_rank: Lotto 1902 says 'officer in the Brunswick army'; English Wikipedia says 'lieutenant in the Prussian army'; NDB 2016 gives the most precise: Seconde-Lieutenant in the Schwarzen Corps. Brunswick army is correct; Prussian is an error.

charlotte_von_girsewald.full_name: Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth von Girsewald (married: Teichmüller)
charlotte_von_girsewald.notes: NDB gives full name Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth. Prior research had 'von Gursewald' (OCR artifact of Lotto 1902). Correct spelling: von Girsewald.

georg_von_girsewald.occupation: Oberstallmeister (Master of the Horse), Hauptmann (Captain), Brunswick court
georg_von_girsewald.notes: Father of Charlotte (Hunter's ancestor). NOT the same as the well-documented Freiherr Conrad Alexander Gustav von Girsewald (1785-1864), who was likely Georg's son or nephew. Both held the title Oberstallmeister across generations.

wilhelm_ernst_conrad_teichmueller.occupation: Oberhütteninspekteur (chief smelting works inspector) at Karlshütte near Delligsen, Leinebergland
wilhelm_ernst_conrad_teichmueller.spouse: Henriette Christiane Helene Schorkopf (1763–1818), from Uslar
wilhelm_ernst_conrad_teichmueller.notes: Karlshütte = ironworks/smelting works. Delligsen is in the Leinebergland (southern Lower Saxony), consistent with the family's historic Harz mining region connections. WikiTree profile for 'Conrad Teichmuller (d. 1835 Holzminden)' and 'Henriette Caroline Luise Teichmuller (b. 1793 Carlshutte)' may be related (Holzminden is near Delligsen; Carlshutte = Karlshütte).

joachim_andreas_teichmueller.role_in_family: Great-great-grandfather of philosopher Gustav; great-grandfather of August Wilhelm (Hunter's ancestor line)
joachim_andreas_teichmueller.notes: Goslar is at the foot of the Harz mountains — consistent with the family origin 'southern Harz' documented back to miller Hans (~1580-1638). An Oberfaktor in Goslar in this era was almost certainly employed in the Harz mining industry (Goslar = historically one of Germany's most important mining centers, home of the Rammelsberg silver mine).

hans_miller_1580.source: NDB 26 (2016) p. 6* — 'Aus seit Ende d. 16. Jh. mit d. Mühlenmeister Han(n)s (um 1580–1638) im südl. Harz nachweisbarer Fam.'
hans_miller_1580.notes: Earliest documented Teichmüller ancestor per the NDB. 'Teichmüller' = pond miller (Teich = pond + Müller = miller). Family is documented in the southern Harz from the end of the 16th century. The surname is occupational.

gustav_teichmueller_philosopher.occupation: Philosopher, professor at University of Basel (1868–1871) and Imperial University of Dorpat (1871–1888)
gustav_teichmueller_philosopher.notable_for: Philosophy of perspectivism influenced Friedrich Nietzsche; founder of Russian personalism; neo-Leibnizianism

children.name: [unnamed, died young]
children.notes: NDB: '1 früh †' among second marriage daughters

children.name: Hertha
children.spouse: Arthur Brückner (1877–1975), ophthalmology professor at Basel

wilhelmina_teichmueller_sister.spouse: Karl Mollenhauer (1818–1864), Protestant pastor and Superintendent at Bockenem/Ambergau
wilhelmina_teichmueller_sister.notes: NEWLY IDENTIFIED in this research. Sister of Gustav and Hans. Not in prior research file 11-teichmueller-german.md. NOTE: Do not confuse with Minette Teichmueller Pohl (1871–1970), who was Hans's DAUGHTER (also nicknamed Minette). Karl Mollenhauer's NDB entry: https://www.deutsche-biographie.de/gnd1154326888.html

wilhelm_teichmueller_brother.spouse: Bertha Kuntzen (NDB) / 'Teichmüller-Specht, Bertha von' (GND index — may be same person with variant name)
wilhelm_teichmueller_brother.possible_son: Robert Teichmüller (1863–1939), pianist — POSSIBLE but unconfirmed

anna_teichmueller_composer.lived_at: Schreiberhau artist colony (Riesengebirge; now Szklarska Poręba, Poland)
anna_teichmueller_composer.associated_with: Carl Hauptmann; composed for over 40 poets

robert_teichmueller_pianist.probable_father: Wilhelm Teichmüller (1834–1869) — POSSIBLE, based on timing and location
robert_teichmueller_pianist.source_for_parentage: Braunschweigisches Biographisches Lexikon (1996) p. 606 — NOT digitized
robert_teichmueller_pianist.hunter_relationship_if_confirmed: 1st cousin 3x removed (if son of Wilhelm, Hans's brother)

notable_collateral_branch.description: Cousin branch sharing ancestor Joachim Andreas (1705-78) via different son Christian Andreas (1734-1809)

joachim_teichmueller_electrotechnician.occupation: Electrical engineer, Karlsruhe TH professor (from 1899), pioneer of Lichtarchitektur (light architecture)
joachim_teichmueller_electrotechnician.founded: Lichttechnisches Institut Karlsruhe (1922) — first such institute in Germany
joachim_teichmueller_electrotechnician.relationship_to_hunter: 4th cousin 3x removed (via shared ancestor Joachim Andreas 1705-78)

confirmed_lineage_hunter.notes: continuation of Mattingly line to Hunter Spence

archion.notes: Subscription required; holds Braunschweig Lutheran records; free index browsing

familysearch.notes: Some Brunswick microfilms digitized; free account required

von_girsewald_source.title: Gothaisches Genealogisches Taschenbuch der Freiherrlichen Häuser (1921), p. 298
von_girsewald_source.notes: Resolves Charlotte's exact position in the von Girsewald family

robert_teichmueller_source.title: Braunschweigisches Biographisches Lexikon, 19. und 20. Jahrhundert (1996), p. 606
robert_teichmueller_source.notes: Cited by German Wikipedia for Robert Teichmüller; would confirm parentage

unresolved_questions: Original parish baptism images not retrieved — NDB dates and parents are confirmed but imaging requires NLA visit
